meta_title: Impact Doors in Tamarac, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Tamarac, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, $0 down financing. Protect your home & save on insurance.
h1: Impact Doors for Tamarac Homes — Built to Outlast Every Storm Season
intro:
Tamarac is a city that knows how to take care of its own. From the manicured fairways of Colony West Country Club to the quiet cul-de-sacs of the Woodlands and Mainlands communities, homeowners here have spent decades building equity and comfort in a place they're proud to call home. But with roughly 55% of Tamarac's housing stock built before 1994 — before Florida's modern hurricane code standards were enacted — a significant portion of those homes are still protected by entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors that were never designed to withstand what South Florida's storm seasons can throw at them. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in bringing those homes into the modern era with code-compliant, hurricane-rated impact doors that deliver real security, real energy savings, and real peace of mind.

Located approximately 8 miles inland from the Atlantic coastline, Tamarac sits in a wind zone rated for 170 mph storm-force winds. While the city falls outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that covers Miami-Dade and portions of southeastern Broward, Florida Building Code still mandates that all new and replacement exterior doors meet hurricane-rated performance standards. That means if your current doors — whether at the front entry of a Tamarac Lakes ranch home or the sliding glass door opening onto a patio off Woodlands Boulevard — haven't been replaced with impact-resistant products, your home may not just be vulnerable to storms. It may also be out of compliance with current code, potentially complicating insurance claims and future sales.

With an average home value of around $300,000, Tamarac homeowners have a lot worth protecting — and a lot to gain from a smart, long-lasting investment like impact doors. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we bring that depth of local experience to every project in Tamarac. Whether you need a single impact entry door for a home near Tamarac Community Center or a complete door package for a multi-entry property in Colony West, our team is ready to deliver the craftsmanship and service that earned us our reputation as The Kings of Service.

section_why.h2: Why Tamarac Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Impact Door Installation
section_why.body:
Choosing the right contractor for impact door installation isn't just about finding the lowest bid — it's about finding a team with the training, licensing, and local knowledge to do the job right the first time. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings 60+ years of combined installation experience to every project, and every single installation is performed by our own in-house crews. We never subcontract. That means the same skilled technicians who show up on day one are the ones pulling the permit with the City of Tamarac Building Division, handling the installation, and completing the final inspection. You're never left wondering who's in your home or whether a third-party crew actually knows what they're doing.

This commitment to in-house installation matters especially in Tamarac, where home styles vary widely across neighborhoods. The split-level homes near Tamarac Lakes have different framing considerations than the single-story villas in the Mainlands, and the large patio sliding doors common in Colony West properties require precise installation to maintain their impact rating. Our crews are trained on the specific products we carry — ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact doors — and understand the manufacturer requirements that must be met to preserve warranty coverage and product certification. A door that's poorly installed, even if it's a top-rated product, can fail to perform when it matters most.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and our reputation is built on transparency and follow-through. We pull every required permit, we schedule every required inspection, and we don't consider a job done until the homeowner is completely satisfied and the documentation is in hand. For Tamarac homeowners who want to protect their investment without the stress of managing a complicated renovation, we make the entire process simple — from free in-home consultation to final sign-off. We're also proud to offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down, making premium impact door protection accessible for households across every part of Tamarac's community.

section_history.h2: Tamarac's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Doors Every Time
section_history.body:
Tamarac has been tested by some of the most powerful storms in modern Florida history, and the lessons from those events are still being felt today. Hurricane Andrew tore through South Florida in August 1992 with catastrophic force, and while Broward County was spared the worst of Andrew's direct impact, the storm exposed just how unprepared the region's older housing stock was for major hurricane forces. Homes throughout the Tamarac area — many built in the 1970s and early 1980s during the city's rapid residential expansion — suffered door failures, blown-in entries, and significant water intrusion through compromised door frames. That storm was the direct catalyst for Florida's sweeping building code reforms, but thousands of pre-1994 Tamarac homes were never retrofitted to meet the new standards.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered a powerful reminder that Broward County is never truly out of the storm's reach. Wilma made landfall near Cape Romano on Florida's southwest coast but tracked northeast with remarkable speed, crossing South Florida as a Category 3 storm and delivering sustained winds that exceeded 100 mph across Broward County. Tamarac residents reported widespread damage to garage doors, entry doors, and sliding glass assemblies — particularly in older neighborhoods where original doors were still in place. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the pattern, tracking up the peninsula and generating prolonged tropical-force wind gusts that tested every exterior opening on homes throughout the region. Since 1990, Tamarac has been impacted by nine named storms — a frequency that makes impact door protection not a luxury, but a baseline requirement for responsible homeownership in this community.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Requirements for Impact Doors in Tamarac
section_code.body:
Tamarac falls under the jurisdiction of the Florida Building Code (FBC) 7th Edition as adopted by Broward County, and all exterior door replacements must comply with the wind-borne debris protection requirements specified for the region's 170 mph design wind speed. While Tamarac is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone — which applies to Miami-Dade County and a strip of southeastern Broward — the city still requires that replacement doors meet impact-resistant standards or be paired with an approved storm protection system. In practice, this means any door installed as a replacement for an existing exterior door must carry valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) documentation and be installed in strict accordance with the manufacturer's approved installation drawing.

The permit process in Tamarac runs through the City of Tamarac Building Division, and every exterior door replacement requires a building permit, product approval documentation, and a final inspection before the project is considered complete. This is a step that many homeowners — and unfortunately, some less-reputable contractors — attempt to skip, either to save time or reduce upfront costs. Skipping the permit process is never a wise shortcut. Unpermitted door installations can trigger complications during home sales, void manufacturer warranties, and in some cases result in mandatory removal and reinstallation at the homeowner's expense. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every aspect of the permit process on your behalf, including submittal of all required product approval documents to the City of Tamarac Building Division and scheduling of the required inspections.

For pre-1994 homes — which represent a majority of Tamarac's housing stock — the installation of new impact doors may also trigger a review of the surrounding opening for compliance with current buck and framing requirements. Our estimators assess these conditions during the initial consultation so there are no surprises once the work begins. Every door we install leaves a clear, documented compliance trail that serves the homeowner for years to come.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Built for Tamarac's Wind Zone
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of the industry's most trusted manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines are engineered and tested to meet or exceed the performance requirements of Florida's strict product approval process, and both carry certifications appropriate for Tamarac's 170 mph wind zone. More importantly, they're built to perform in the real-world conditions that Tamarac homeowners face — not just sustained hurricane-force winds, but the daily punishment of South Florida's intense UV exposure, salt-laden air, and heavy seasonal rainfall.

Our impact entry doors from ESW and CWI feature laminated glass inserts with a shatter-resistant interlayer that holds the glass in place even under direct impact from wind-borne debris. The door frames are constructed from heavy-duty reinforced materials and paired with multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously — a critical feature that dramatically increases resistance to forced entry as well as wind pressure loading. For sliding glass doors, which are among the most vulnerable openings in any Tamarac home and a common upgrade in Colony West and Tamarac Lakes properties, our product lines offer smooth-gliding systems with interlocking panels and robust frame construction that maintains its integrity under load without warping or racking over time.

French door configurations are also available for homeowners who want the aesthetic of a traditional paired door without sacrificing storm performance. Both ESW and CWI French door systems feature reinforced astragal assemblies and multi-point locking hardware that ensure the doors function as a unified, impact-rated assembly rather than two independent panels. All products are available in a range of finishes and glass options, allowing Tamarac homeowners to upgrade their storm protection without compromising the curb appeal of their home.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Tamarac — And Why the Investment Makes Sense
section_cost.body:
For Tamarac homes averaging around $300,000 in value, impact door installation represents one of the highest-return upgrades a homeowner can make. The cost of a single impact entry door installation — including the door unit, hardware, permit, and professional installation — typically ranges from $1,500 to $3,500 depending on door size, configuration, and glass package selected. Sliding glass door replacements generally range from $2,500 to $5,500 per unit for standard residential sizes, while French door packages fall in a similar range. A complete door package for a typical Tamarac home might include an impact front entry door, a sliding glass rear door, and one or two secondary entries, with total investment ranging from $5,000 to $12,000 depending on the scope of work and product selections.

For homeowners in Tamarac's lower-middle income range who are managing a household budget carefully, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing solutions designed to make these upgrades achievable without financial strain. Our financing options start at $87 per month with $0 down through approved credit, meaning you can have professionally installed, code-compliant impact doors protecting your home from the very next storm season without a large upfront cash outlay. We work with our financing partners to find terms that fit your situation, and our team can walk you through the options during your free in-home estimate.

The return on this investment is realized in multiple ways. Insurance premium reductions — often ranging from 10% to 25% on the wind portion of a Broward County homeowner's policy — can yield meaningful annual savings that compound over the life of the doors. Energy efficiency gains from the improved thermal performance of impact door assemblies can reduce monthly cooling costs. And when the time comes to sell, impact-upgraded homes in Tamarac consistently command stronger buyer interest and appraisal value, particularly as more buyers understand the cost and complexity of retrofitting a home with code-compliant storm protection after purchase.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Tamarac Homeowners Who Install Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and measurable financial benefits of installing impact doors in Tamarac is the reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums — and in today's Florida insurance market, where premiums have risen sharply for properties across Broward County, those savings carry real weight. When you replace non-impact doors with fully rated impact-resistant doors from ESW or CWI, you become eligible for wind mitigation credits under Florida's Uniform Mitigation Verification Inspection form. These credits apply specifically to the wind coverage portion of your policy, which typically represents the largest component of a South Florida homeowner's annual premium.

Insurance carriers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and various private market carriers — are required by Florida law to offer premium discounts for verified wind mitigation features. Impact-rated doors that meet the opening protection requirements outlined on the wind mitigation inspection form can qualify your home for significant savings, especially when combined with impact windows on the same property. Tamarac homeowners who complete a full opening protection upgrade — replacing all exterior doors and windows with impact-rated products — often see the most dramatic premium reductions, as the home qualifies for the highest tier of wind mitigation credit available.

To take advantage of these savings, you'll need a licensed wind mitigation inspector to complete and submit the standardized inspection form to your insurance carrier. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing strongly recommends scheduling this inspection immediately after your impact door installation is complete and the permit has been finaled by the City of Tamarac Building Division. We can provide all the product documentation and installation records your inspector will need to verify the opening protection credits. In many cases, Tamarac homeowners find that the annual insurance savings alone begin recovering the cost of their impact door investment within just a few years.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors if Tamarac isn't in the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone?
faqs.a: Yes. While Tamarac falls outside the HVHZ, the city sits in a 170 mph wind zone and is subject to Florida Building Code requirements mandating hurricane-rated opening protection for all exterior door replacements. With over half of Tamarac's homes built before 1994's code reforms, and a documented history of storm impacts from Andrew, Wilma, and Irma, impact doors are both a code requirement and a practical necessity for protecting your home and family.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation cost in Tamarac, and do you offer financing?
faqs.a: Costs vary by door type and configuration. A single impact entry door typically runs $1,500 to $3,500 installed, while sliding glass doors range from $2,500 to $5,500. A full door package for a typical Tamarac home can range from $5,000 to $12,000. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down through approved credit, making it easier for Tamarac homeowners to get hurricane-rated protection without a large upfront payment.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit from the City of Tamarac to replace my exterior doors?
faqs.a: Yes. All exterior door replacements in Tamarac require a building permit through the City of Tamarac Building Division, along with Florida Product Approval documentation and a final inspection. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application to final sign-off — so you don't have to navigate the bureaucracy alone. Never accept an installation from a contractor who offers to skip the permit process, as this creates significant legal and insurance complications.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take for a typical Tamarac home?
faqs.a: Most standard impact door installations are completed in one to two days once permits are approved. The permit approval timeline through the City of Tamarac Building Division typically runs one to three weeks depending on current volume. After your free in-home estimate, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will handle product ordering, permit submittal, and scheduling — most Tamarac homeowners go from signed contract to completed installation within four to eight weeks. We use only our own in-house crews, so there are no subcontractor scheduling delays.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact doors in Tamarac?
faqs.a: Savings vary by carrier and the scope of your upgrade, but Broward County homeowners who install impact-rated doors and qualify for wind mitigation credits commonly see reductions of 10% to 25% on the wind portion of their premium. When impact doors are combined with impact windows for full opening protection, savings can be even more substantial. A licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your new doors and submit the required form to your carrier — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all supporting documentation to streamline that process.

cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to put our 60+ years of combined experience and A+ BBB reputation to work for your Tamarac home — call us today or fill out our quick online form to schedule your free, no-pressure in-home consultation.
